title: chrysophyllum oppositum
intro: Chrysophyllum oppositum, also known as the West African star apple, is a tropical tree native to West Africa that produces a fruit with a star-shaped cross-section. The medicinal properties of Chrysophyllum oppositum include its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of consuming the fruit include its high nutritional value, providing essential vitamins and minerals, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory properties. Chrysophyllum oppositum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits when consumed.
